progress_update.summary: We scoped and planned the Unified Health SQLite Database from scratch. Ran /spectre:scope to define three pillars (podcast health knowledge, blood labs, Oura Ring), then /spectre:plan to produce a full comprehensive implementation plan with 4 phases and 26 sub-tasks. Along the way we reorganized all project planning docs out of the ironclaw repo into ~/Code Projects/{Project Name}/ — Health DB, Podcast Feed, Kindle Highlights, and Ollama Bootstrap all have homes there now. Session ended with approved scope, approved plan, and a complete tasks.md ready for /spectre:execute.
progress_update.goal: Build health.db — a unified SQLite database replacing health_knowledge.json and adding blood lab history + Oura Ring biometrics. Success: cross-pillar query 'how has my A1c changed and what does Attia say about it' returns a meaningful answer.
accomplished: Scoped three-pillar health DB (podcast knowledge + blood labs + Oura Ring) via /spectre:scope — scope.md saved to ~/Code Projects/Health DB/concepts/scope.md
Ran /spectre:plan: 4 parallel research agents (finder, analyst, patterns, web-research) produced comprehensive task_context.md; architectural options presented and Option A (swap-in place) selected
Generated full implementation plan (plan.md) with 4 phases, complete SQLite schema, Oura v2 API patterns, Excel unpivot approach, launchd plist design — approved by Jeff
Generated tasks.md with 26 sub-tasks, dependency analysis, sequential and 8-wave parallel execution strategies
Reorganized all project planning docs from ironclaw/docs/tasks/ into ~/Code Projects/ — committed removal from repo (c2d74b3); saved future-pillars roadmap and deferred features to memory
progress_update.now: Session ended just after /spectre:handoff was invoked. Ready to start /spectre:execute on Phase 1 (Foundation) in the next session.
next_steps: Run /spectre:execute to implement Phase 1: create health_db.py, migrate_health_knowledge.py, modify health_store.py, create health_store_cmd.py, retire health_knowledge.json
Phase 2 (blood labs) gated on Jeff providing the Excel file
Phase 3 (Oura) gated on Jeff obtaining PAT from https://cloud.ouraring.com/personal-access-tokens
After Phases 2+3, run Phase 4 cross-pillar validation query
constraints: health_store.py public API (append_entry, load_all, find_by_show) must be preserved — engine.py and on_demand.py cannot change
DB lives at podcast_vault/health.db inside the mounted workspace — accessible from both host (python3.13) and container (python3)
All host scripts use /usr/local/bin/python3.13; deps installed via pip3.13
No jq in container — all JSON handling in skill scripts must use python3
FTS5 must be verified available in both host and container Python before implementing
decisions: Option A (swap-in place): health_store.py internals replaced with SQLite, public API unchanged — zero changes to callers
launchd oura sync uses StartCalendarInterval (batch/weekly) not KeepAlive (daemon) — different from whisper-server pattern
Blood lab import runs --dry-run first to handle unknown Excel layout quirks before committing
Jeff has Gen3 Oura ring — pull all endpoints including stress, resilience, VO2 max; handle 404s gracefully
blockers: Blood lab import blocked until Jeff provides the Excel file
Oura historical import blocked until Jeff obtains PAT from https://cloud.ouraring.com/personal-access-tokens
risks: FTS5 may not be compiled into the container's Python sqlite3 — must verify before implementing triggers
Blood test Excel may have layout quirks (merged cells, non-standard headers) — --dry-run mode designed to surface these
